summary: Added a Python ELF postprocessor and wired it into the C object build rule. The tool supports three CodeWarrior-oriented fixes: ctor/dtor section alignment repair, mangled-symbol character substitution reversal, and old-stack prologue/epilogue scheduling fixups. The Makefile now invokes python3 tools/postprocess.py after each C compilation with -fprologue-fixup=old_stack, and adds -nodefaults to CFLAGS.

key_files.reason: New 318-line Python ELF postprocessor implementing ctor realignment, C++ symbol fixup, and old_stack epilogue instruction reordering.
Adds PYTHON and POSTPROC variables, adds -nodefaults to CFLAGS, defines PROCFLAGS, and runs the postprocessor after compiling each .c file to .o.

what_changed: Introduced tools/postprocess.py as an executable Python 3 script for in-place ELF object patching.
Documented the postprocessor as supporting three decompilation-related CodeWarrior patches: -fctor-realign, -fsymbol-fixup, and -fprologue-fixup=old_stack.

Added symbol substitution helpers mapping irregular mangled-name characters to placeholder sequences: '<' to '?0', '>' to '?1', '@' to '?2', '\\' to '?3', ',' to '?4', and '-' to '?5', with decode logic to restore them in ELF string tables.
Added binary stream helpers for big-endian u8/u16/u32 reads and u32 writes, matching PowerPC/ELF data handling needs.
Implemented .ctors/.dtors section alignment patch discovery by walking section headers and looking up section names through the section-header string table.

Implemented ELF string-table symbol fixup by scanning non-section-name string tables and scheduling string replacements padded with zero bytes when decoded names are shorter than their placeholder form.

Implemented old_stack epilogue fixup for .text by locating mtlr r0 instruction 0x7C0803A6 before blr instruction 0x4E800020, then shifting intervening instructions up and placing mtlr immediately before blr.
Added assertions around text section alignment, instruction stream assumptions, unmatched mtlr detection, and maximum mtlr-to-blr distance.
Changed Makefile CFLAGS from '-Cpp_exceptions off -proc gekko -fp hard -O4,p $(INCLUDES)' to include '-nodefaults'.
Added Makefile variables PYTHON := python3 and POSTPROC := tools/postprocess.py.
Added PROCFLAGS := -fprologue-fixup=old_stack and invokes '$(PYTHON) $(POSTPROC) $(PROCFLAGS) $@' after each C compile.
smart_moves: Kept compiler-specific binary corrections outside source files by adding a post-build object-file patching step, avoiding pollution of decompiled C with workaround-only constructs.
Made the postprocessor flag-driven, with default-disabled features for ctor realignment and symbol fixup and an explicit prologue-fixup mode, making later build configurations easier to tune.
Used section-header parsing instead of hardcoded offsets for .text, .ctors, .dtors, and string-table handling, which is more reusable across object files.
Deferred symbol-table string rewriting until after scanning by collecting ToReplace records, then applied changes to a byte array with length checks and zero padding.
Encoded the old_stack fix as a narrow, conservative epilogue transformation guarded by instruction-pattern checks and assertions about alignment and maximum scheduling distance.
Integrated the tool into the generic %.o: %.c Makefile rule so every C object receives consistent postprocessing automatically.
decomp_lessons: For old CodeWarrior matching, byte-for-byte object matching may require post-compilation ELF surgery when the available compiler version schedules epilogues differently from the target compiler.

When compiler or assembler tools cannot represent certain mangled C++ symbol characters directly, a placeholder naming scheme can be used during assembly/compilation and reversed in the ELF string table afterward.
Build-system hooks can be used to normalize compiler output without changing every source file; here, the postprocessor is attached to the pattern rule for C compilation.
ELF section headers and section-name string tables provide enough structure to write targeted object patches without relying entirely on fixed file offsets.
Binary patch tools should make assumptions explicit. This script asserts .text offset and size alignment, instruction alignment, no lone mtlr, and bounded mtlr/blr separation.
For ctor/dtor alignment bugs, patching section-header alignment fields can be a practical workaround when the compiler emits incorrect alignment metadata.
A small, repository-local postprocessing tool can capture project-specific compiler quirks and make those quirks reproducible for all contributors.
naming_conventions: Makefile variable for the Python interpreter is named PYTHON.
Makefile variable for the postprocessor path is named POSTPROC.
Makefile variable for postprocessor flags is named PROCFLAGS.

Postprocessor feature flags follow compiler-like '-f...' and '-fno-...' forms, including '-fctor-realign', '-fno-ctor-realign', '-fsymbol-fixup', '-fno-symbol-fixup', and '-fprologue-fixup=old_stack'.
The C++ symbol placeholder convention maps irregular characters to question-mark IDs: '?0' for '<', '?1' for '>', '?2' for '@', '?3' for '\\', '?4' for ',', and '?5' for '-'.
The old CodeWarrior scheduler/prologue mode is named 'old_stack'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Run an ELF postprocessor after mwcceppc.exe emits each object file to correct compiler-version differences before linking.
Use '-nodefaults' in CFLAGS alongside CodeWarrior options '-Cpp_exceptions off -proc gekko -fp hard -O4,p'.
For old_stack epilogue matching, identify 'mtlr r0' as machine word 0x7C0803A6 and 'blr' as 0x4E800020, then move mtlr down to immediately precede blr while shifting intervening instructions upward.
Skip zero instructions as padding during .text scanning.
Treat .text as 4-byte-aligned instruction-only content and assert offset and size are multiples of 4.

For symbol fixups, scan ELF string tables, skip the first SHT_STRTAB occurrence as the section-name table, decode placeholder names in later string tables, and patch strings in place with zero padding.
For ctor/dtor alignment, locate .ctors and .dtors by section name and patch their section header alignment field to 4.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify tools/postprocess.py or change PROCFLAGS.
Search for uses of -fctor-realign or -fsymbol-fixup in later Makefile/build-system changes.
Look for later discussion of old_stack, mtlr/blr epilogue matching, or CodeWarrior scheduler differences.
Check whether postprocess.py was replaced, split, or ported to another build tool in later project history.
Search for object matching fixes involving .ctors, .dtors, or C++ mangled symbol placeholder substitution.
